methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Layup Parts is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zack Eakin that has raised $2M+. The broader robotics and manufacturing sector contains one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The stage-progression method is applicable and forms the sole quantitative anchor for this analysis. Using the $2M total capital raised as a proxy for seed-stage valuation and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier to the next financing stage yields an implied valuation of $5M. A peer-median approach cannot be applied; while eleven robotics and manufacturing companies exist in the broader dataset, zero same-stage comparables, zero adjacent-stage comparables, and zero similar-tier peers meet the threshold for a statistically meaningful narrow peer set (≥3 datapoints required).

The triangulated valuation range is $4M–$6M, with a median of $5M. This range reflects reasonable bounds around the single applicable method, acknowledging structural uncertainty. Key constraints on precision include the absence of stage-matched peers in the sector and reliance on a raised-capital proxy rather than direct comparable transaction multiples. Any refinement of this range would require either identifying additional seed-stage robotics companies with disclosed valuations or observing Layup's performance relative to disclosed metrics of the peer universe.
